questions.stem: Quill Co. uses a periodic FIFO system. Beginning inventory: 400 units @ $9. Purchases: 600 units @ $11 and 500 units @ $13. During the period, 1,000 units were sold. What is the cost of goods sold?
questions.explanation: Under periodic FIFO, the earliest costs are assigned to COGS. Units sold = 1,000. Layers consumed: 400 units @ $9 = $3,600, then 600 units @ $11 = $6,600. Total = 400 + 600 = 1,000 units. COGS = $3,600 + $6,600 = $10,200. (Ending inventory of 500 units @ $13 = $6,500 remains.)

questions.subtopic: Periodic LIFO - Cost of Goods Sold
questions.stem: Sterling Co. uses periodic LIFO. Beginning inventory: 300 units @ $20. Purchases during the year: 400 units @ $24 and 500 units @ $27. Units sold during the year: 800. What is the cost of goods sold under periodic LIFO?
questions.explanation: Under periodic LIFO, the most recent costs are assigned to COGS. Units sold = 800. Layers consumed from newest first: 500 units @ $27 = $13,500, then 300 units @ $24 = $7,200. Total = 500 + 300 = 800 units. COGS = $13,500 + $7,200 = $20,700. (Ending inventory = 300 @ $20 + 100 @ $24 = $6,000 + $2,400 = $8,400 remains.)

questions.stem: Vega Co. purchases merchandise with a list price of $20,000 subject to trade discounts of 20% and 10% (chain discount). Credit terms are 2/10, n/30, and Vega does not intend to pay within the discount period. Using the gross method, at what amount should the purchase be recorded?
questions.explanation: Trade (chain) discounts are applied sequentially to the list price and are never recorded; only the net invoice price is recorded. Invoice price = $20,000 × (1 − 0.20) × (1 − 0.10) = $20,000 × 0.80 × 0.90 = $14,400. Because the gross method is used and the cash discount is not expected to be taken, the purchase is recorded at the full invoice price of $14,400 (the 2% cash discount is recognized only if taken).
questions.explanationJa: 取引（連鎖）割引は定価に順次適用され、記帳されない。記録するのは正味送り状価格のみ。送り状価格 = $20,000 × (1 − 0.20) × (1 − 0.10) = $20,000 × 0.80 × 0.90 = $14,400。総額法で現金割引を受けない見込みのため、仕入は送り状価格全額$14,400で記録（2%の現金割引は受けた場合のみ認識）。

questions.subtopic: Vendor Rebate / Cash Consideration from Vendor
questions.stem: Marlin Co. purchases 50,000 units of a component at $12 each from a supplier. The purchase agreement provides a volume rebate of $0.50 per unit once annual purchases exceed 40,000 units, and Marlin expects to earn the full rebate. Under ASC 705-20, what is the cost recorded per unit of inventory?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 705-20, cash consideration received from a vendor (such as a volume rebate) is presumed to be a reduction of the price of the goods purchased and therefore reduces the cost of inventory, unless it is a payment for a distinct good or service or a reimbursement of a specific cost. The rebate reduces unit cost: $12.00 − $0.50 = $11.50 per unit.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 705-20により、仕入先から受け取る現金対価（数量リベート等）は、別個の財・サービスの対価または特定費用の補填でない限り、購入した財の価格の減額と推定され、棚卸資産の原価を減額する。リベートで単価が減少：$12.00 − $0.50 = $11.50。

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Interest and Storage - Cost Exclusion
questions.stem: Aspen Co. purchases inventory for $300,000. It also incurs $4,000 of interest on a general short-term loan used partly for operations, $6,000 of routine warehouse storage costs for finished goods awaiting sale, and $5,000 of freight-in. Under ASC 330, what amount should be capitalized as inventory cost?
questions.explanation: Inventory cost includes purchase price ($300,000) and freight-in ($5,000) because freight-in brings the goods to their present location and condition. General interest on routine inventory is a period cost and is not capitalized (interest is capitalized only for discrete projects that take substantial time to get ready, per ASC 835-20). Routine storage of finished goods is a period selling/holding cost, not inventoriable. Capitalized inventory cost = $300,000 + $5,000 = $305,000.
questions.explanationJa: 棚卸資産原価には購入価格($300,000)と仕入運賃($5,000)を含める（運賃は商品を現在の場所・状態にするため）。通常在庫の一般利息は期間費用で資産化しない（利息はASC 835-20の準備に相当の時間を要する個別プロジェクトのみ資産化）。完成品の通常保管費は期間費用で棚卸不可。資産化原価 = $300,000 + $5,000 = $305,000。

questions.stem: Beacon Mfg. budgeted fixed manufacturing overhead of $480,000 based on normal capacity of 120,000 machine hours. During the period only 90,000 machine hours were used due to a labor strike, and actual fixed overhead was $480,000. How much fixed overhead must be expensed as a period cost (rather than capitalized to inventory)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 330-10-30-3, fixed overhead is allocated using normal capacity. Fixed overhead rate = $480,000 / 120,000 hours = $4.00 per hour. Amount allocated to inventory = 90,000 actual hours × $4.00 = $360,000. The remaining unallocated fixed overhead from operating below normal capacity = $480,000 − $360,000 = $120,000 must be expensed in the period as a period charge.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 330-10-30-3により、固定間接費は正常生産能力で配賦。固定間接費率 = $480,000 / 120,000時間 = $4.00/時間。在庫への配賦額 = 90,000実際時間 × $4.00 = $360,000。正常能力を下回る操業で未配賦となる固定間接費 = $480,000 − $360,000 = $120,000を当期費用として計上する。

questions.stem: Larkin Co. adopts dollar-value LIFO. At the end of Year 1, the ending inventory extended at current-year (Year 1) costs is $264,000, and the same physical inventory extended at base-year costs is $240,000. The base-year layer (January 1) was $240,000 at an index of 1.00. What is the internally computed price index for Year 1, and the DV-LIFO ending inventory?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Index 1.10; DV-LIFO $240,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Index 1.10; DV-LIFO $264,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Index 0.91; DV-LIFO $240,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Index 1.10; DV-LIFO $254,400

questions.explanation: Under the double-extension method, the current-year price index = Ending inventory at current-year cost / Ending inventory at base-year cost = $264,000 / $240,000 = 1.10. Ending inventory at base-year cost ($240,000) equals the base layer ($240,000), so there is no new layer this year. The DV-LIFO inventory therefore remains the base layer of $240,000 × 1.00 = $240,000.
questions.explanationJa: 二重価格法では、当年度物価指数 = 当年度原価による期末在庫 / 基準年度原価による期末在庫 = $264,000 / $240,000 = 1.10。基準年度原価による期末在庫($240,000)は基礎層($240,000)に等しく、新規層はない。よってDV-LIFO在庫は基礎層$240,000 × 1.00 = $240,000のまま。

questions.stem: Monroe Co. uses dollar-value LIFO. Base-year inventory (index 1.00) = $200,000. End of Year 1: inventory at base = $230,000, index 1.05. End of Year 2: inventory at base = $260,000, index 1.12. What is the DV-LIFO inventory at the end of Year 2?

During a period of rising prices, Crestview Co. would report COGS of $720,000 under FIFO and $760,000 under LIFO. The income tax rate is 21%. Compared with FIFO, how much does adopting LIFO reduce the company's current income tax expense?
questions.explanation: LIFO produces higher COGS in a period of rising prices, lowering pretax income. The reduction in pretax income = LIFO COGS − FIFO COGS = $760,000 − $720,000 = $40,000. The tax savings = reduction in pretax income × tax rate = $40,000 × 21% = $8,400. This lower tax payment is the cash-flow advantage of LIFO during inflation.
questions.explanationJa: 物価上昇期にLIFOはCOGSが高くなり税引前利益を引き下げる。税引前利益の減少 = LIFO COGS − FIFO COGS = $760,000 − $720,000 = $40,000。税金節約額 = 税引前利益の減少 × 税率 = $40,000 × 21% = $8,400。この税金支払減少が物価上昇期のLIFOのキャッシュフロー上の利点である。

questions.subtopic: Lump-Sum Purchase with One Unknown Fair Value
questions.stem: Dalton Co. acquires a bundle of three product lines for a single price of $480,000. Reliable fair values are available for two: Line A $300,000 and Line B $180,000. The fair value of Line C cannot be reliably determined, but management estimates it at $120,000. Cost is allocated on relative fair value. How much cost is allocated to Line C?
questions.explanation: In a lump-sum (basket) purchase, the total cost is allocated to each item in proportion to its relative fair value. Total fair value = $300,000 + $180,000 + $120,000 = $600,000. Line C allocation = ($120,000 / $600,000) × $480,000 = 0.20 × $480,000 = $96,000.

questions.stem: Felton Co. uses a perpetual moving-average system. Beginning inventory: 400 units @ $15. May 4: purchased 200 units @ $18. May 9: sold 300 units. May 18: purchased 300 units @ $20. What is the ending inventory balance after the May 18 purchase?
questions.explanation: After May 4: total cost = (400 × $15) + (200 × $18) = $6,000 + $3,600 = $9,600; units = 600; average = $9,600 / 600 = $16.00. May 9 sale of 300 units at $16.00 = $4,800 COGS; remaining = 300 units × $16.00 = $4,800. After May 18 purchase: total cost = $4,800 + (300 × $20) = $4,800 + $6,000 = $10,800; units = 600. Ending inventory = $10,800.

questions.stem: Hale Mfg. uses a standard cost system. Standard direct labor per unit: 3 hours @ $20/hour. During the month, 2,000 units were produced using 5,800 actual labor hours at an actual rate of $21/hour. What is the direct labor efficiency (quantity) variance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $4,000 favorable

choices.label: B
choices.text: $5,800 unfavorable

choices.label: C
choices.text: $4,000 unfavorable

choices.label: D
choices.text: $4,200 favorable

questions.explanation: Standard hours allowed = 2,000 units × 3 hours = 6,000 hours. Labor efficiency variance = (Actual hours − Standard hours allowed) × Standard rate = (5,800 − 6,000) × $20 = (−200) × $20 = $4,000 favorable (fewer hours were used than the standard allowed). The rate variance, by contrast, is (Actual rate − Standard rate) × Actual hours = ($21 − $20) × 5,800 = $5,800 unfavorable, but the question asks only for the efficiency variance.
questions.explanationJa: 標準許容時間 = 2,000単位 × 3時間 = 6,000時間。労務能率差異 = (実際時間 − 標準許容時間) × 標準賃率 = (5,800 − 6,000) × $20 = (−200) × $20 = $4,000有利（標準より少ない時間で生産）。なお賃率差異は ($21 − $20) × 5,800 = $5,800不利だが、設問は能率差異のみを問う。

questions.subtopic: Goods in Transit - Freight Capitalization with FOB
questions.stem: On December 31, Kingsley Co. has goods costing $60,000 in transit from a vendor, shipped FOB shipping point on December 28, with freight of $1,200 to be paid by Kingsley. Separately, Kingsley shipped $40,000 of goods to a customer FOB shipping point on December 30. What amount related to these items should be in Kingsley's December 31 inventory?
questions.explanation: Incoming goods shipped FOB shipping point: title passed to Kingsley on December 28, so the $60,000 belongs in inventory even though in transit, and the $1,200 freight-in is an inventoriable cost (buyer pays freight on FOB shipping point). Inventory for incoming goods = $60,000 + $1,200 = $61,200. Outgoing goods shipped to a customer FOB shipping point: title passed to the customer at shipment on December 30, so the $40,000 is no longer Kingsley's inventory. Total = $61,200.
questions.explanationJa: FOB出荷地点の仕入品：所有権は12/28にKingsleyへ移転、輸送中でも$60,000は在庫に含め、運賃$1,200は棚卸原価（FOB出荷地点では買主が運賃負担）。仕入品在庫 = $60,000 + $1,200 = $61,200。顧客宛FOB出荷地点の出荷品：所有権は12/30の出荷時に顧客へ移転、$40,000はKingsleyの在庫ではない。合計 = $61,200。

questions.subtopic: Sale with Right of Return - Inventory Returned Asset
questions.stem: Norwood Co. sells 5,000 units at $50 each (cost $30 each) and, based on history, expects 4% of units to be returned. Under ASC 606, in addition to a refund liability, Norwood recognizes an asset for its right to recover products from customers. At what amount is that asset recorded (assume no recovery costs or impairment)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-23 through 55-29, a seller that expects returns recognizes (1) revenue and a refund liability only for units not expected to be returned, and (2) an asset (and corresponding adjustment to cost of sales) for its right to recover products, measured at the former carrying amount of the inventory less any expected recovery costs. Expected returned units = 5,000 × 4% = 200 units. Right-to-recover asset = 200 × $30 cost = $6,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-23〜55-29により、返品が見込まれる売手は、(1) 返品見込みのない単位についてのみ収益と返金負債を認識し、(2) 製品回収権の資産（および売上原価の対応調整）を、棚卸資産の従前簿価から回収予想費用を控除した額で認識する。返品見込数 = 5,000 × 4% = 200単位。回収権資産 = 200 × $30 = $6,000。

questions.stem: Using the net method, Ridge Co. records a $40,000 purchase with terms 1/15, n/45. Before payment, Ridge returns $5,000 (gross list amount) of defective goods. What is the inventory balance for this purchase after the return, assuming the goods are still unpaid and within the discount period?
questions.explanation: Under the net method, inventory is recorded net of the available discount. Net purchase = $40,000 × (1 − 0.01) = $39,600. The return is also recorded net: $5,000 × (1 − 0.01) = $4,950. Inventory after return = $39,600 − $4,950 = $34,650. Equivalently, the net amount payable = ($40,000 − $5,000) × 0.99 = $35,000 × 0.99 = $34,650.

questions.subtopic: Conventional Retail Method - Ending Inventory at Cost
questions.stem: Hudson Stores uses the conventional retail method (lower of cost or market). Beginning inventory: cost $60,000, retail $90,000. Net purchases: cost $300,000, retail $420,000. Net markups $30,000. Net markdowns $20,000. Net sales $400,000. What is the estimated ending inventory at cost?
questions.explanation: Conventional retail excludes net markdowns from the cost-to-retail ratio. Cost-to-retail ratio = (Cost: $60,000 + $300,000) / (Retail: $90,000 + $420,000 + $30,000) = $360,000 / $540,000 = 66.67%. Ending inventory at retail = $90,000 + $420,000 + $30,000 − $20,000 − $400,000 = $120,000. Ending inventory at cost = $120,000 × 66.67% = $80,000.

questions.subtopic: Cost Retail Method - Average Cost (Markdowns Included)
questions.stem: Pinehill Co. uses the cost (average) retail method, which includes both net markups and net markdowns in the cost-to-retail ratio. Beginning inventory: cost $48,000, retail $80,000. Net purchases: cost $192,000, retail $304,000. Net markups $30,000. Net markdowns $14,000. Net sales $296,000. What is the estimated ending inventory at cost?
questions.explanation: The cost (average) retail method includes both net markups and net markdowns in the cost-to-retail ratio. Cost-to-retail ratio = (Cost: $48,000 + $192,000) / (Retail: $80,000 + $304,000 + $30,000 − $14,000) = $240,000 / $400,000 = 60%. Ending inventory at retail = $400,000 − $296,000 sales = $104,000. Ending inventory at cost = $104,000 × 60% = $62,400.

questions.subtopic: Gross Profit Method - Margin Stated on Cost
questions.stem: A flood destroyed Tilden Co.'s inventory. Records show beginning inventory $90,000, net purchases $410,000, and net sales $600,000. The company normally earns a gross profit of 25% on cost. What is the estimated cost of the inventory destroyed?
questions.explanation: Gross profit of 25% on cost means COGS = Sales / 1.25. Estimated COGS = $600,000 / 1.25 = $480,000. Goods available for sale = $90,000 + $410,000 = $500,000. Estimated ending (destroyed) inventory = $500,000 − $480,000 = $20,000. Note: 25% on cost equals a 20% gross margin on sales ($600,000 × 0.80 = $480,000), giving the same COGS.

questions.stem: Quartz Co. uses weighted-average process costing. Beginning WIP: 2,000 units (40% complete as to conversion). Started: 18,000 units. Completed and transferred out: 16,000 units. Ending WIP: 4,000 units (75% complete as to conversion). Materials are added at the beginning of the process. What are the equivalent units of production for conversion costs?
questions.explanation: Under the weighted-average method, equivalent units = units completed and transferred out + (ending WIP × percent complete). Beginning WIP completion percentage is ignored under weighted average. Conversion EUP = 16,000 (completed) + (4,000 × 75%) = 16,000 + 3,000 = 19,000 equivalent units.
questions.explanationJa: 加重平均法では、完成換算量 = 完成・振替単位 + (期末仕掛品 × 完成度)。加重平均法では期首仕掛品の完成度は無視する。加工費の完成換算量 = 16,000（完成）+ (4,000 × 75%) = 16,000 + 3,000 = 19,000単位。

questions.subtopic: Purchase Commitment - Recovery in Following Year
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Oakdale Co. accrued a $30,000 loss on a noncancelable commitment to buy 10,000 units at $15 (market had fallen to $12). The goods are received in February Year 2 when the market price has recovered to $13. At what amount should the inventory be recorded upon receipt in Year 2?
questions.explanation: When the loss was accrued at Year 1 end, the carrying basis was effectively written down to the December 31 market of $12. When the goods are received in Year 2, inventory is recorded at the lower of the contract price ($15) and the current market at receipt ($13) = $13 per unit. Inventory = 10,000 × $13 = $130,000. The recovery from $12 to $13 reduces the previously accrued loss (a $10,000 gain/recovery in Year 2), but inventory cannot exceed the current market of $13. Entry: Dr Inventory $130,000, Dr Accrued loss liability $30,000, Cr Accounts payable $150,000, Cr Recovery of loss $10,000.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1末に損失を計上した時点で、簿価は12/31の市場価格$12まで実質的に評価減された。Year 2の受領時、在庫は契約価格($15)と受領時の市場価格($13)のいずれか低い方 = $13で記録する。在庫 = 10,000 × $13 = $130,000。$12から$13への回復は前期計上損失を減額する（Year 2に$10,000の戻入益）が、在庫は市場価格$13を超えられない。仕訳：（借）在庫$130,000、見越損失負債$30,000 /（貸）買掛金$150,000、損失戻入$10,000。

questions.stem: Riverton Co. manufactures a main product and a minor by-product. Total joint costs are $250,000, none of which is allocated to the by-product. During the period the by-product is sold for $9,000 with $1,500 of selling costs. Under the realized-value (other income) method, where the by-product's net proceeds are recognized as other income when sold, what net cost is charged to the main product?
questions.explanation: Under the realized-value (other income) method for by-products, no joint cost is allocated to the by-product, and the by-product's net realized value is recognized as other income (or as a reduction of COGS) only when sold — it does not reduce the joint cost assigned to the main product. Therefore the full joint cost of $250,000 is charged to the main product. (The by-product's net proceeds of $9,000 − $1,500 = $7,500 are shown separately as other income.) This contrasts with the NRV method, which would reduce the main product cost by the by-product's NRV.
questions.explanationJa: 副産物の実現価値（雑収入）法では、副産物に連産品原価を配賦せず、副産物の正味実現価額は販売時に雑収入（またはCOGSの減額）として認識するのみで、主産物に配賦する連産品原価を減額しない。よって連産品原価$250,000全額が主産物に賦課される。（副産物の正味手取$9,000 − $1,500 = $7,500は別途雑収入として表示。）NRV法では主産物原価が副産物NRV分減額される点で異なる。

questions.stem: Selby Co. (consignor) ships 1,000 units costing $40 each to a consignee and pays $3,000 of freight to deliver them. By year-end the consignee has sold 600 units. What amount of consignment inventory should Selby report on its year-end balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Consigned goods remain the consignor's inventory until sold by the consignee, and freight paid to deliver goods to the consignee is an inventoriable cost allocated to all units shipped. Unsold units = 1,000 − 600 = 400. Product cost of unsold units = 400 × $40 = $16,000. Freight allocated to unsold units = ($3,000 × 400/1,000) = $1,200. Consignment inventory = $16,000 + $1,200 = $17,200.
questions.explanationJa: 委託品は受託者が販売するまで委託者の在庫であり、受託者への配送運賃は出荷全単位に配分される棚卸原価である。未販売数 = 1,000 − 600 = 400。未販売分の製品原価 = 400 × $40 = $16,000。未販売分への配分運賃 = $3,000 × 400/1,000 = $1,200。委託在庫 = $16,000 + $1,200 = $17,200。

questions.subtopic: Inventory Write-Down Recovery Within Same Year (Allowance)
questions.stem: At the end of Q1, Granger Co. recorded an inventory write-down of $50,000 using an allowance account when NRV fell below cost. By the end of Q2 (same fiscal year), NRV partially recovered, and the required allowance is now only $18,000. The inventory had not been sold. What entry is appropriate at the end of Q2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Allowance to Reduce Inventory to NRV $32,000; Credit Recovery of Inventory Loss $32,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No entry; U.S. GAAP prohibits any reversal of inventory write-downs

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Inventory $50,000; Credit Recovery of Inventory Loss $50,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Recovery of Inventory Loss $18,000; Credit Allowance to Reduce Inventory to NRV $18,000

questions.explanation: U.S. GAAP prohibits recognizing inventory above original cost, but recoveries of write-downs taken earlier in the same fiscal year through an allowance account are permitted as interim adjustments because the annual measurement governs. The allowance must be reduced from $50,000 to $18,000, a $32,000 recovery. Entry: Dr Allowance to Reduce Inventory to NRV $32,000; Cr Recovery of Inventory Loss $32,000. The recovery is limited to amounts previously written down within the year and never raises inventory above original cost.
questions.explanationJa: 米国GAAPは在庫を元の原価超で計上することを禁じるが、同一会計年度内に評価性引当金を用いて計上した評価減の戻入れは、年次測定が支配するため中間調整として認められる。引当金を$50,000から$18,000へ$32,000減額する。仕訳：（借）在庫評価減引当金$32,000 /（貸）在庫評価損戻入$32,000。戻入れは年度内に評価減した範囲に限られ、元の原価を超えない。

questions.subtopic: Inventoriable Cost - Sales Tax and Installation
questions.stem: Wexler Co. buys merchandise for resale with an invoice cost of $90,000, pays nonrefundable sales tax of $4,500 on the purchase, $2,000 of freight-in, and $1,200 to clean its sales floor for the month. What amount should be capitalized as inventory cost?
questions.explanation: Inventory cost includes the invoice price, nonrefundable purchase taxes, and freight-in, because all are necessary to bring the goods to their present location and condition. Inventoriable cost = $90,000 + $4,500 (nonrefundable sales tax) + $2,000 (freight-in) = $96,500. The $1,200 to clean the sales floor is a period selling/operating cost and is not inventoriable.
questions.explanationJa: 棚卸資産原価には送り状価格、還付されない購入税、仕入運賃を含める。これらは商品を現在の場所・状態にするために必要だからである。棚卸原価 = $90,000 + $4,500（還付不能売上税）+ $2,000（仕入運賃）= $96,500。売場清掃の$1,200は期間販売費・営業費で棚卸不可。

questions.subtopic: Raw Materials Write-Down When Finished Goods Below Cost
questions.stem: Ashby Mfg. holds raw materials costing $70,000 with a current replacement cost of $58,000. The finished goods that will incorporate these materials are now expected to sell below their production cost. At what amount should the raw materials be reported under ASC 330?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 330-10-35-4, raw materials are not written down below cost if the finished goods in which they will be used are expected to sell at or above cost. However, when the related finished goods are expected to be sold below cost, the raw materials should be written down, and replacement cost is the best measure of NRV for raw materials. Therefore the raw materials are reported at replacement cost of $58,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 330-10-35-4により、原材料が使用される完成品が原価以上で販売される見込みなら原材料を原価以下に評価減しない。しかし関連完成品が原価未満で販売される見込みの場合は原材料を評価減し、原材料のNRVの最良の指標は再調達原価である。よって原材料は再調達原価$58,000で報告する。

questions.subtopic: Weighted Average vs FIFO - Ending Inventory Difference
questions.stem: Tremont Co. began the year with no inventory. It purchased 1,000 units @ $10, then 1,000 units @ $14, and sold 1,200 units during the year using a periodic system. By how much does ending inventory under FIFO exceed ending inventory under weighted average?
questions.explanation: Units available = 2,000; sold = 1,200; ending units = 800. FIFO ending inventory: 800 units from the most recent purchase @ $14 = $11,200. Weighted average cost = (1,000 × $10 + 1,000 × $14) / 2,000 = $24,000 / 2,000 = $12 per unit; WA ending inventory = 800 × $12 = $9,600. Difference = $11,200 − $9,600 = $1,600. FIFO is higher because, in rising prices, FIFO leaves the newest higher-cost units in ending inventory.
questions.explanationJa: 利用可能数 = 2,000、販売 = 1,200、期末数 = 800。FIFO期末在庫：最新仕入の800単位@$14 = $11,200。加重平均単価 = (1,000×$10 + 1,000×$14) / 2,000 = $12。加重平均期末在庫 = 800 × $12 = $9,600。差額 = $11,200 − $9,600 = $1,600。物価上昇期はFIFOが新しい高原価単位を期末在庫に残すため高くなる。

questions.subtopic: Inventory Turnover - Gross vs Net Method Trap
questions.stem: Calder Co. reports net sales of $2,000,000, cost of goods sold of $1,200,000, beginning inventory of $140,000, and ending inventory of $160,000. What is the inventory turnover ratio?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 8.0 times

choices.label: B
choices.text: 13.3 times

choices.label: C
choices.text: 7.5 times

choices.label: D
choices.text: 12.5 times

questions.explanation: Inventory turnover = Cost of goods sold / Average inventory. Average inventory = ($140,000 + $160,000) / 2 = $150,000. Inventory turnover = $1,200,000 / $150,000 = 8.0 times. Using net sales of $2,000,000 in the numerator (a common error) would incorrectly give 13.3 times; turnover must use COGS, not sales.
questions.explanationJa: 在庫回転率 = 売上原価 / 平均在庫。平均在庫 = ($140,000 + $160,000) / 2 = $150,000。在庫回転率 = $1,200,000 / $150,000 = 8.0回。分子に純売上$2,000,000を使う誤りでは13.3回となる。回転率は売上ではなくCOGSを用いる。

questions.subtopic: Standard Cost - Disposition of Immaterial Variance
questions.stem: At year-end, Whitman Co.'s standard cost system shows a net unfavorable variance of $9,000 that management considers immaterial. Ending inventory at standard cost is $250,000 and COGS at standard cost is $1,000,000. How should the immaterial variance be reported under ASC 330?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Charge the entire $9,000 to cost of goods sold

choices.label: B
choices.text: Allocate $1,800 to inventory and $7,200 to COGS

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalize the entire $9,000 to ending inventory

choices.label: D
choices.text: Report $9,000 as a separate nonoperating loss

questions.explanation: Under ASC 330-10-30-3, standard costs are acceptable for inventory if they approximate actual cost. When variances are immaterial, they may be charged entirely to cost of goods sold in the period rather than prorated between inventory and COGS. Proration (allocating between inventory and COGS) is required only when variances are material. Because this $9,000 variance is immaterial, it is charged in full to COGS.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 330-10-30-3により、標準原価が実際原価に近似する場合は棚卸に使用できる。差異が重要でない場合は、在庫とCOGSに比例配分せず、その期のCOGSに全額賦課してよい。比例配分は差異が重要な場合にのみ必要。本件の$9,000は重要でないためCOGSに全額賦課する。

questions.stem: Vail Co. applies LCNRV to total inventory in the aggregate. Item 1: cost $120,000, NRV $110,000. Item 2: cost $80,000, NRV $96,000. Item 3: cost $50,000, NRV $44,000. What is the required write-down under the total-inventory (aggregate) approach?
questions.explanation: Under the aggregate (total inventory) approach, total cost is compared with total NRV, allowing increases on some items to offset decreases on others. Total cost = $120,000 + $80,000 + $50,000 = $250,000. Total NRV = $110,000 + $96,000 + $44,000 = $250,000. Because total NRV ($250,000) is not below total cost ($250,000), no write-down is required. The aggregate method is the least conservative LCNRV grouping.
questions.explanationJa: 総額（全体）法では総原価と総NRVを比較し、一部の含み益が他の含み損を相殺できる。総原価 = $120,000 + $80,000 + $50,000 = $250,000。総NRV = $110,000 + $96,000 + $44,000 = $250,000。総NRV($250,000)が総原価($250,000)を下回らないため評価減は不要。総額法はLCNRVのグルーピングで最も保守的でない。

questions.stem: Monarch Appliances removes 30 refrigerators (cost $600 each) from saleable inventory to use as long-term showroom display models expected to be used for about three years before disposal. How should these units be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify $18,000 to property, plant and equipment and depreciate over the display period

choices.label: B
choices.text: Continue to report the $18,000 as inventory until ultimately sold

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense the $18,000 immediately as a marketing cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reclassify $18,000 to a prepaid asset and amortize over one year

questions.explanation: Inventory is held for sale in the ordinary course of business. Items withdrawn for use by the entity over multiple periods (such as long-term demonstration or display units used for about three years) no longer meet the definition of inventory; they are used in operations and should be reclassified to property, plant and equipment and depreciated over their useful life as display assets. Cost reclassified = 30 × $600 = $18,000.
questions.explanationJa: 棚卸資産は通常の事業過程で販売目的に保有される。複数期間（約3年の長期展示・実演など）にわたり企業自身が使用する目的で引き出された品目はもはや棚卸資産の定義を満たさず、営業用に使用されるため有形固定資産に振り替え、展示資産として耐用年数で減価償却する。振替原価 = 30 × $600 = $18,000。

questions.subtopic: Gross Profit Method - Solving for Ending Inventory with Returns
questions.stem: Pelham Co. estimates inventory using the gross profit method. Beginning inventory $110,000; purchases $520,000; purchase returns $20,000; freight-in $10,000; gross sales $760,000; sales returns $40,000. The gross profit margin is 30% of net sales. What is the estimated ending inventory?

On January 1, Hartley Co. transfers inventory with a carrying amount of $92,000 to a financial institution for $100,000 cash and simultaneously agrees to repurchase the same inventory on June 30 for $106,000. Under ASC 606, what amount of interest expense should Hartley recognize over the six-month period related to this arrangement?
questions.explanation: Because Hartley is obligated to repurchase the inventory at a price ($106,000) that is equal to or greater than the original selling price ($100,000), the arrangement is a financing, not a sale. Hartley keeps the inventory on its books, records a $100,000 financial liability for the cash received, and recognizes the $6,000 excess of the repurchase price over the cash received ($106,000 − $100,000) as interest expense over the six months. $14,000 incorrectly adds the $8,000 difference between the cash received and the carrying amount; $8,000 is the difference between cash received and the inventory's carrying amount, which is not interest; $0 would be appropriate only if the transfer qualified as a sale.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-68では、買戻価格が当初の販売価格以上であるフォワード契約付きの譲渡は売却ではなく金融取引（product financing）として処理します。Hartleyは棚卸資産を帳簿に残したまま、受取現金$100,000を金融負債として計上し、買戻価格$106,000と受取額$100,000の差額$6,000を6か月にわたり支払利息として認識します。$14,000は帳簿価額との差額$8,000を誤って加算した値、$8,000は受取現金と帳簿価額の差額であり利息ではありません。売却処理が認められる場合のみ利息は$0となります。

questions.subtopic: Ending Inventory - Cutoff and Ownership Adjustments
questions.stem: Brandt Co.'s physical inventory count on December 31 totaled $500,000. The count did not include $35,000 of goods purchased FOB shipping point that were shipped by the vendor on December 30 and were still in transit. The count also excluded $22,000 of goods sold to a customer FOB destination that were in transit at year-end. The count included $18,000 of goods held on consignment from a supplier. What amount should Brandt report as inventory on December 31?
questions.explanation: Goods purchased FOB shipping point belong to the buyer once shipped, so the $35,000 in transit must be added. Goods sold FOB destination remain the seller's property until delivery, so the $22,000 in transit must also be added. Goods held on consignment belong to the consignor, so the $18,000 must be removed. $500,000 + $35,000 + $22,000 − $18,000 = $539,000. $517,000 omits the FOB destination sale in transit; $575,000 incorrectly adds the consigned goods instead of removing them; $504,000 omits the FOB shipping point purchase and mishandles the consigned goods.
questions.explanationJa: FOB shipping point（出荷地渡し）の仕入は出荷時点で買手に所有権が移転するため、輸送中の$35,000を加算します。FOB destination（仕向地渡し）の売上は引渡しまで売手の資産なので、輸送中の$22,000も加算します。委託者（consignor）から預かった受託在庫$18,000は自社の資産ではないため除外します。$500,000 + $35,000 + $22,000 − $18,000 = $539,000（ASC 330-10）。$517,000はFOB destination売上分の加算漏れ、$575,000は受託在庫を誤って加算、$504,000はFOB shipping point仕入分の加算漏れによる誤りです。

questions.stem: Which of the following items should be excluded from Dunn Co.'s December 31 inventory?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Goods purchased FOB shipping point that are in transit from the vendor at year-end

choices.label: B
choices.text: Goods shipped to a dealer on consignment that remain unsold at year-end

choices.label: C
choices.text: Goods sold to a customer FOB destination that are in transit at year-end

choices.label: D
choices.text: Goods held in Dunn's warehouse on consignment from a manufacturer

questions.explanation: Goods held on consignment from a manufacturer belong to the consignor, not to Dunn, so they are excluded from Dunn's inventory even though they are physically in Dunn's warehouse. Goods purchased FOB shipping point belong to Dunn once the vendor ships them; goods out on consignment remain Dunn's property until the consignee sells them; and goods sold FOB destination remain Dunn's property until they reach the customer. All three of those items are properly included in Dunn's inventory.
questions.explanationJa: 受託品（メーカーから預かったconsignment在庫）の所有権は委託者にあるため、物理的にDunnの倉庫にあってもDunnの棚卸資産から除外します。一方、FOB shipping pointで仕入れた輸送中の商品は出荷時点でDunnの資産となり、委託販売のため販売店へ出荷した未販売品はDunnの資産のままであり、FOB destinationで販売した輸送中の商品も顧客への引渡しまでDunnの資産です。これら3つはすべてDunnの棚卸資産に含めます。

questions.stem: Keller Imports purchased merchandise for resale with an invoice cost of $60,000. Keller also incurred the following costs: freight-in of $3,000, insurance on the goods while in transit of $1,200, import duties of $2,400, and advertising for the goods of $5,000. What amount should Keller record as the cost of the inventory?
questions.explanation: Inventory cost includes all costs directly incurred to bring the goods to their existing condition and location: invoice cost $60,000 + freight-in $3,000 + transit insurance $1,200 + import duties $2,400 = $66,600. Advertising is a selling expense and is never inventoried. $63,000 includes only freight-in and omits insurance and duties; $64,200 omits the import duties; $71,600 incorrectly capitalizes the $5,000 of advertising.
questions.explanationJa: 棚卸資産の取得原価には、商品を現在の場所・状態に至らせるために直接要したすべての費用を含めます（ASC 330-10-30-1）。送り状価額$60,000 + 引取運賃$3,000 + 輸送中保険料$1,200 + 輸入関税$2,400 = $66,600です。広告宣伝費は販売費であり棚卸資産に算入できません。$63,000は保険料と関税の算入漏れ、$64,200は関税の算入漏れ、$71,600は広告費$5,000を誤って資産化した値です。

questions.stem: Which inventory cost flow method produces the same dollar amount of ending inventory regardless of whether a periodic or a perpetual recording system is used?

choices.label: A
choices.text: FIFO

choices.label: B
choices.text: LIFO

choices.label: C
choices.text: Weighted average

choices.label: D
choices.text: All cost flow methods produce identical results under both systems

questions.explanation: Under FIFO, the oldest costs are always assigned to cost of goods sold first, so ending inventory always consists of the most recent costs whether cost of goods sold is computed at each sale (perpetual) or at period-end (periodic). LIFO produces different results because perpetual LIFO assigns the most recent costs available at each sale date, while periodic LIFO uses the most recent costs of the entire period. The weighted average method also differs: the periodic system computes one average for the period, while the perpetual (moving average) system recomputes the average after each purchase. Therefore the statement that all methods produce identical results is false.
questions.explanationJa: FIFOでは最も古い原価から順に売上原価へ配分されるため、売上の都度計算する継続記録法でも期末に一括計算する周期棚卸法でも、期末棚卸資産は常に最新の原価で構成され同額になります。LIFOは、継続記録法では各販売時点で入手可能な最新原価を、周期法では期間全体の最新原価を払い出すため結果が異なります。加重平均法も、周期法は期間全体で1つの平均を、継続記録法（移動平均法）は仕入の都度平均を再計算するため異なります。したがって「すべての方法で同一」という記述は誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Perpetual LIFO vs Periodic LIFO - Ending Inventory
questions.stem: Voss Co. uses perpetual LIFO. Transactions for March: beginning inventory 100 units at $10; March 1 purchase 100 units at $12; March 15 sale of 150 units; March 20 purchase 100 units at $14. What is Voss's ending inventory at March 31?
questions.explanation: Under perpetual LIFO, the March 15 sale of 150 units uses the most recent costs available on that date: 100 units at $12 ($1,200) plus 50 units at $10 ($500), leaving 50 units at $10. After the March 20 purchase, ending inventory is 50 units at $10 ($500) plus 100 units at $14 ($1,400) = $1,900. $1,600 is the periodic LIFO ending inventory (100 at $10 + 50 at $12), which would be wrong because the March 20 purchase occurred after the sale; $2,000 is the FIFO ending inventory (100 at $14 + 50 at $12); $1,700 is the cost of goods sold, not the ending inventory.
questions.explanationJa: 継続記録LIFOでは、3月15日の販売150個にはその時点で入手可能な最新原価を充てます。$12の100個（$1,200）と$10の50個（$500）を払い出し、$10の50個が残ります。3月20日の仕入後、期末棚卸資産は$10×50個（$500）＋$14×100個（$1,400）＝$1,900です。$1,600は周期LIFOの期末在庫（$10×100＋$12×50）であり、販売後に行われた3月20日仕入を払出に充てている点で誤り。$2,000はFIFOの期末在庫、$1,700は売上原価です。

questions.stem: Ralston Co. uses the weighted-average cost method with a periodic inventory system. Beginning inventory was 200 units at $5.00. Purchases during the year were 300 units at $6.00 and 500 units at $6.60. Ending inventory is 250 units. What is the cost of Ralston's ending inventory?
questions.explanation: Total cost of goods available for sale is (200 × $5.00) + (300 × $6.00) + (500 × $6.60) = $1,000 + $1,800 + $3,300 = $6,100 for 1,000 units, giving a weighted-average cost of $6.10 per unit. Ending inventory is 250 × $6.10 = $1,525. $1,467 results from using a simple average of the three unit prices ($5.87) instead of weighting by quantity; $1,300 is the periodic LIFO ending inventory (200 at $5.00 + 50 at $6.00); $1,650 is the FIFO ending inventory (250 at $6.60).
questions.explanationJa: 周期加重平均法では、販売可能総原価（200×$5.00）＋（300×$6.00）＋（500×$6.60）＝$6,100を総数量1,000個で割り、加重平均単価$6.10を算定します。期末棚卸資産は250個×$6.10＝$1,525です。$1,467は数量で加重せず3つの単価の単純平均（$5.87）を使った誤り、$1,300は周期LIFOの期末在庫（$5.00×200個＋$6.00×50個）、$1,650はFIFOの期末在庫（$6.60×250個）に相当します。

questions.subtopic: Moving Average - Unit Cost After Successive Purchases
questions.stem: Ingram Co. uses a perpetual inventory system with the moving-average method. Beginning inventory was 100 units at $8.00. Ingram then purchased 300 units at $10.00, sold 200 units, and finally purchased 200 units at $11.00. What is the moving-average unit cost after the final purchase?
questions.explanation: After the first purchase, the average is ($800 + $3,000) ÷ 400 units = $9.50. The sale of 200 units removes 200 × $9.50 = $1,900, leaving 200 units at $9.50 ($1,900). After the final purchase, the new average is ($1,900 + $2,200) ÷ 400 units = $10.25. $9.67 is the simple average of the three unit prices and ignores quantities and the timing of the sale; $9.50 is the average before the final purchase; $11.00 is only the cost of the last purchase.
questions.explanationJa: 移動平均法では仕入の都度平均単価を再計算します。最初の仕入後は（$800＋$3,000）÷400個＝$9.50。200個の販売で200×$9.50＝$1,900が払い出され、残高は200個・$1,900です。最後の仕入後は（$1,900＋$2,200）÷400個＝$10.25となります。$9.67は数量を無視した3つの単価の単純平均、$9.50は最終仕入前の平均単価、$11.00は最終仕入の単価のみを用いた誤りです。

questions.stem: Corbin Co. uses unit LIFO and has the following layers at the beginning of the year: base layer 1,000 units at $5; Year 1 layer 400 units at $7; Year 2 layer 200 units at $9. During the current year, sales exceeded purchases by 500 units, and the current-year replacement cost was $12 per unit throughout the year. By what amount did the LIFO liquidation increase Corbin's pretax income?
questions.explanation: The 500-unit decrease liquidates the most recent layers first: all 200 units of the Year 2 layer at $9 and 300 units of the Year 1 layer at $7. Each liquidated unit is charged to cost of goods sold at its old layer cost instead of the $12 current cost, so pretax income increases by (200 × ($12 − $9)) + (300 × ($12 − $7)) = $600 + $1,500 = $2,100. $1,500 considers only the Year 1 layer portion; $3,500 incorrectly assumes all 500 units came from the $5 base layer; $2,500 incorrectly applies a $5 average cost difference to all 500 units.
questions.explanationJa: LIFOでは在庫数量が減少すると新しい層から順に清算されます。500個の減少は、Year 2層200個（@$9）全部とYear 1層300個（@$7）を清算します。清算分は当期仕入原価$12ではなく旧層の低い原価で売上原価に計上されるため、税引前利益は（200×($12−$9)）＋（300×($12−$7)）＝$600＋$1,500＝$2,100増加します。$1,500はYear 1層のみを考慮した値、$3,500は500個全部を基礎層原価$5から清算したと誤認した値、$2,500は500個全部に一律$5の差額を適用した誤りです。

questions.stem: Which of the following statements correctly describes the LIFO conformity rule?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An entity that uses LIFO for financial reporting must also use LIFO for income tax purposes

choices.label: B
choices.text: An entity that uses LIFO for income tax purposes must also use LIFO in its primary financial statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: An entity must use the same LIFO pooling structure for tax and financial reporting

choices.label: D
choices.text: An entity using LIFO for tax purposes is prohibited from disclosing non-LIFO supplemental information

questions.explanation: Under IRC Section 472(c), a taxpayer may use LIFO for tax purposes only if it also uses LIFO in its primary financial statements to report income to shareholders and creditors — the requirement runs from tax to book, not from book to tax. An entity may freely use LIFO for books without electing it for tax. The rule does not require identical pooling structures, and supplemental non-LIFO disclosures (such as the LIFO reserve or FIFO-equivalent inventory) are expressly permitted in notes to the financial statements.
questions.explanationJa: 内国歳入法第472条(c)のLIFO一致規則（LIFO conformity rule）は、税務上LIFOを採用する納税者は株主・債権者向けの主たる財務諸表でもLIFOを使用しなければならないと定めています。要求の方向は「税務→会計」であり、会計上LIFOを使っても税務上の採用義務はありません。税務と会計でプーリング構造を一致させる必要はなく、LIFOリザーブやFIFO換算額などの補足開示は注記で明示的に認められています。

questions.stem: Pryor Co. adopted dollar-value LIFO when its inventory was $300,000 (index 1.00). At the end of Year 1, inventory at current-year cost was $352,000 and the index was 1.10. At the end of Year 2, inventory at current-year cost was $420,000 and the index was 1.20. What is Pryor's dollar-value LIFO inventory at the end of Year 2?
questions.explanation: Year 1: $352,000 ÷ 1.10 = $320,000 at base-year cost, creating a layer of $20,000 × 1.10 = $22,000. Year 2: $420,000 ÷ 1.20 = $350,000 at base-year cost, creating a new layer of $30,000 × 1.20 = $36,000. Dollar-value LIFO inventory = $300,000 + $22,000 + $36,000 = $358,000. $350,000 is the Year 2 inventory at base-year cost without restating the layers to their own indexes; $420,000 is the unadjusted current-year cost; $360,000 incorrectly prices both layers at the Year 2 index of 1.20.
questions.explanationJa: ドルバリューLIFOでは、各年の期末在庫を基準年価格に引き直して数量的増加を把握し、増加層をその年の指数で再評価します。Year 1：$352,000÷1.10＝$320,000、新層$20,000×1.10＝$22,000。Year 2：$420,000÷1.20＝$350,000、新層$30,000×1.20＝$36,000。合計は$300,000＋$22,000＋$36,000＝$358,000です。$350,000は層を各年の指数で再評価しない基準年価格のまま、$420,000は当期原価のまま、$360,000は両方の層を誤ってYear 2の指数1.20で評価した値です。

questions.stem: Selwyn Co. uses dollar-value LIFO with the double-extension method. At year-end, ending inventory consists of 10,000 units. The base-year cost is $20 per unit and the current-year cost is $23 per unit. What price index should Selwyn use for the current year?
questions.explanation: Under the double-extension method, the ending inventory is extended (priced) twice — once at current-year cost and once at base-year cost — and the index is the ratio of the two: (10,000 × $23) ÷ (10,000 × $20) = $230,000 ÷ $200,000 = 1.15. 0.87 inverts the ratio by dividing base-year cost by current-year cost; 1.10 and 1.20 do not correspond to the $23/$20 cost relationship in the data.
questions.explanationJa: 二重延長法（double-extension method）では、期末在庫数量を当年原価と基準年原価の両方で延長計算し、その比率を価格指数とします。（10,000個×$23）÷（10,000個×$20）＝$230,000÷$200,000＝1.15です。0.87は分子と分母を逆にした（基準年原価÷当年原価）誤り、1.10や1.20は$23と$20の原価関係からは導かれない値です。

questions.subtopic: Dollar-Value LIFO - Advantage over Unit LIFO
questions.stem: Compared with the traditional unit LIFO method, the primary advantage of the dollar-value LIFO method is that dollar-value LIFO:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reduces the likelihood of LIFO layer liquidation because new items can replace discontinued items within a pool

choices.label: B
choices.text: Eliminates the need to compute a price index for the inventory

choices.label: C
choices.text: Values ending inventory at current replacement cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Always produces a higher ending inventory than unit LIFO during inflation

questions.explanation: Dollar-value LIFO measures inventory in pools of dollars restated to base-year prices rather than in physical units of specific items. Because a pool is maintained in total dollars, a decline in one item can be offset by an increase in another item in the same pool, so old layers are less likely to be liquidated when the product mix changes. Dollar-value LIFO requires a price index (it does not eliminate one); it values inventory at restated historical layer costs, not replacement cost; and it does not systematically produce a higher ending inventory than unit LIFO.
questions.explanationJa: ドルバリューLIFOは個々の品目の物量単位ではなく、基準年価格に引き直した金額ベースのプール単位で在庫を測定します。プール全体の金額で層を維持するため、ある品目の減少を同一プール内の別品目の増加で相殺でき、品目構成が変わっても旧層の清算（LIFO liquidation）が起こりにくいことが最大の利点です。価格指数の算定はむしろ必要であり、評価額は再評価後の歴史的層原価であって再調達原価ではなく、単位LIFOより常に期末在庫が大きくなるわけでもありません。

questions.subtopic: LCM vs LCNRV - Applicability by Cost Method
questions.stem: Under ASC 330, as amended by ASU 2015-11, the lower of cost or market (LCM) rule — with its ceiling and floor limits on replacement cost — applies to inventory measured using which cost methods?

choices.label: A
choices.text: FIFO and weighted average only

choices.label: B
choices.text: LIFO and the retail inventory method only

choices.label: C
choices.text: All cost flow methods

choices.label: D
choices.text: Specific identification and moving average only

questions.explanation: ASU 2015-11 simplified subsequent measurement for inventory measured using FIFO, average cost, specific identification, and similar methods: those inventories are measured at the lower of cost or net realizable value (LCNRV). Inventory measured using LIFO or the retail inventory method was scoped out of the simplification and continues to apply the traditional lower of cost or market rule, where market is replacement cost subject to a ceiling (NRV) and a floor (NRV less a normal profit margin).
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2015-11により、FIFO・平均原価法・個別法等で測定する棚卸資産は低価法の比較対象が正味実現可能価額（NRV）に簡素化されました（LCNRV）。一方、LIFOおよび小売棚卸法（retail inventory method）で測定する棚卸資産はこの簡素化の適用範囲外とされ、従来どおり再調達原価を上限（NRV）と下限（NRV−正常利益）で制限する低価法（LCM）を適用し続けます（ASC 330-10-35-1C）。

questions.subtopic: Lower of Cost or Market - Replacement Cost Above Ceiling
questions.stem: Marsden Co. uses LIFO and applies the lower of cost or market rule to an inventory item with the following data: cost $64; replacement cost $62; estimated selling price $74; estimated costs to complete and sell $14; normal profit margin $12. At what amount should Marsden report this item?
questions.explanation: The ceiling is net realizable value: $74 − $14 = $60. The floor is NRV less normal profit: $60 − $12 = $48. Market is replacement cost ($62) limited to the ceiling-floor range; because $62 exceeds the $60 ceiling, market is $60. The item is reported at the lower of cost ($64) or market ($60), which is $60. $62 fails to limit replacement cost to the ceiling; $64 is cost, which exceeds market; $48 is the floor, which is only used when replacement cost falls below it.
questions.explanationJa: LIFO採用のため従来のLCMを適用します。上限（ceiling）はNRV＝$74−$14＝$60、下限（floor）はNRV−正常利益＝$60−$12＝$48です。市場価額は再調達原価$62を上限・下限の範囲内に制限した値であり、$62は上限$60を超えるため市場価額は$60となります。原価$64と市場価額$60の低い方の$60で評価します。$62は上限による制限を行わない誤り、$64は市場価額を上回る原価、$48は再調達原価が下限を下回る場合にのみ使用する下限値です。

questions.stem: Talbot Co. uses FIFO and holds 400 units of a product with a cost of $200 per unit. At year-end, the estimated selling price is $210 per unit and estimated costs to sell are $25 per unit. What loss, if any, should Talbot recognize on this inventory at year-end?
questions.explanation: Because Talbot uses FIFO, the inventory is measured at the lower of cost or net realizable value. NRV is $210 − $25 = $185 per unit, which is below the $200 cost, so each unit is written down by $15. The total loss is 400 × $15 = $6,000. $0 incorrectly compares cost only to the $210 selling price without deducting costs to sell; $10,000 uses the full $25 selling cost per unit as the write-down; $4,000 does not correspond to the cost-NRV difference of $15 per unit.
questions.explanationJa: FIFO採用のためLCNRVを適用します（ASC 330-10-35-1B）。NRV＝売価$210−販売費用$25＝$185が原価$200を下回るため、1個あたり$15の評価減が必要です。損失合計は400個×$15＝$6,000です。$0は販売費用を控除せず売価$210と原価を比較した誤り、$10,000は販売費用$25全額を評価減額とした誤り、$4,000は1個あたりの原価とNRVの差額$15から導かれない値です。

questions.stem: Crandall Co. measures its inventory using the FIFO method. In applying the lower of cost or net realizable value test under ASC 330, what role does current replacement cost play?

choices.label: A
choices.text: None — cost is compared directly with net realizable value, and replacement cost is not considered

choices.label: B
choices.text: Replacement cost is the measure of market, subject to a ceiling and floor

choices.label: C
choices.text: Replacement cost is used only when it exceeds net realizable value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Replacement cost is used as the floor below which inventory cannot be written down

questions.explanation: For inventory measured using FIFO, average cost, or other non-LIFO, non-retail methods, ASU 2015-11 replaced the lower of cost or market framework with a direct comparison of cost to net realizable value (estimated selling price less reasonably predictable costs of completion, disposal, and transportation). Replacement cost, the ceiling, and the floor are no longer part of the test for these inventories. The ceiling-and-floor framework that uses replacement cost as market survives only for LIFO and retail-method inventories.
questions.explanationJa: FIFOや平均原価法など（LIFO・小売棚卸法以外）で測定する棚卸資産については、ASU 2015-11により従来のLCMの枠組みが廃止され、原価を正味実現可能価額（見積売価−完成・処分・運送に要する合理的に予測可能な費用）と直接比較するLCNRVに置き換えられました。再調達原価や上限・下限の概念はこのテストでは一切使用しません。再調達原価を市場価額とする上限・下限の枠組みは、LIFOおよび小売棚卸法の在庫にのみ残存しています。

questions.subtopic: Conventional Retail Method - Ending Inventory Computation
questions.stem: Garfield Stores uses the conventional retail inventory method (lower of cost or market). Data for the year: beginning inventory cost $26,000, retail $40,000; net purchases cost $174,000, retail $350,000; net markups $10,000; net markdowns $30,000; net sales $310,000. What is Garfield's estimated ending inventory at cost?
questions.explanation: Under the conventional method, the cost-to-retail ratio excludes net markdowns: ($26,000 + $174,000) ÷ ($40,000 + $350,000 + $10,000) = $200,000 ÷ $400,000 = 50%. Ending inventory at retail is goods available at retail of $400,000 (beginning retail $40,000 + purchases retail $350,000 + markups $10,000), less markdowns $30,000, less sales $310,000 = $60,000. Ending inventory at cost is $60,000 × 50% = $30,000. $32,432 results from incorrectly deducting markdowns in the ratio denominator ($200,000 ÷ $370,000 = 54.05% × $60,000); $60,000 is the ending inventory at retail, not at cost; $36,000 results from using a 60% ratio that has no support in the data.
questions.explanationJa: コンベンショナル（従来法・低価法近似）小売棚卸法では、原価率の分母に純値上げ（net markups）は含めるが純値下げ（net markdowns）は含めません。原価率＝（$26,000＋$174,000）÷（$40,000＋$350,000＋$10,000）＝$200,000÷$400,000＝50%。期末売価在庫＝売価ベース販売可能額$400,000−値下げ$30,000−売上$310,000＝$60,000。期末原価在庫＝$60,000×50%＝$30,000です。$32,432は値下げを分母に含めた原価率54.05%を使った誤り、$60,000は売価ベースの在庫、$36,000はデータから導かれない60%の原価率による値です。

questions.stem: In applying the retail inventory method, how should normal shortage (shrinkage) be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included in the cost-to-retail ratio denominator

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ignored entirely until a physical count confirms it

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deducted in the retail column in arriving at ending inventory at retail, but excluded from the cost-to-retail ratio

choices.label: D
choices.text: Added to net sales at cost in the cost column

questions.explanation: Normal shortage (breakage, theft, shrinkage expected in the ordinary course of business) reduces the goods that remain on hand, so it is deducted in the retail column — after the cost-to-retail ratio has been computed — in arriving at ending inventory at retail. It is not included in the ratio because shortage relates to goods no longer available, and including it would distort the relationship between cost and retail of the goods available for sale. Ignoring shortage until a count would overstate estimated ending inventory, and shortage is a retail-column item, not a cost-column addition to sales.
questions.explanationJa: 正常な減耗（万引き・破損等、通常の営業過程で見込まれるshrinkage）は手許に残る商品を減少させるため、原価率算定後に売価欄から控除して期末売価在庫を求めます。原価率の計算には含めません。減耗はもはや販売可能でない商品に関するものであり、比率に含めると販売可能商品の原価と売価の関係が歪むためです。実地棚卸まで無視すると推定期末在庫が過大になり、また減耗は売価欄の控除項目であって原価欄で売上に加算する項目でもありません。

questions.subtopic: Gross Profit Method - Casualty Loss with Salvage
questions.stem: A tornado destroyed Whitfield Co.'s warehouse on September 30. Records show beginning inventory of $60,000, net purchases through September 30 of $240,000, and net sales through September 30 of $350,000. Whitfield's gross profit rate is 30% of sales. Undamaged goods with a cost of $15,000 were recovered, and damaged goods were sold for scrap proceeds of $4,000. What is Whitfield's estimated inventory casualty loss?
questions.explanation: Cost of goods available for sale is $60,000 + $240,000 = $300,000. Estimated cost of goods sold is $350,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $245,000, so estimated inventory at the date of the tornado is $300,000 − $245,000 = $55,000. The loss is reduced by the $15,000 of undamaged goods and the $4,000 of scrap recoveries: $55,000 − $15,000 − $4,000 = $36,000. $55,000 is the total estimated inventory before recoveries; $40,000 deducts only the undamaged goods; $51,000 deducts only the scrap proceeds.
questions.explanationJa: 売上総利益法により、販売可能原価＝$60,000＋$240,000＝$300,000、推定売上原価＝$350,000×（1−30%）＝$245,000、災害時点の推定在庫＝$300,000−$245,000＝$55,000です。損失額は回収された無傷の商品$15,000とスクラップ売却収入$4,000を控除し、$55,000−$15,000−$4,000＝$36,000となります。$55,000は回収分控除前の推定在庫全額、$40,000は無傷商品のみ控除、$51,000はスクラップ収入のみ控除した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Inventory Error - Purchase and Inventory Both Omitted
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Lockhart Co. failed to record a $10,000 purchase of goods shipped FOB shipping point that were in transit at year-end. The goods were also excluded from the Year 1 physical count and ending inventory. The purchase and the goods were recorded when they arrived in January, Year 2. What is the effect of this error on Lockhart's Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income is correctly stated, but inventory and accounts payable are both understated

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net income is understated and inventory is understated

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net income is overstated and accounts payable is understated

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net income, inventory, and accounts payable are all correctly stated

questions.explanation: Cost of goods sold equals beginning inventory plus purchases minus ending inventory. Because both purchases and ending inventory are understated by the same $10,000, the two errors offset and cost of goods sold — and therefore net income — is correctly stated. However, the balance sheet is misstated: inventory is understated by $10,000 and accounts payable is understated by $10,000. An effect on net income would arise only if just one of the two items (the purchase or the ending inventory) had been omitted.
questions.explanationJa: 売上原価＝期首在庫＋仕入−期末在庫です。仕入と期末在庫の両方が同額$10,000ずつ過少なため、誤りは相殺され売上原価と当期純利益は正しく表示されます。しかし貸借対照表では棚卸資産が$10,000過少、買掛金が$10,000過少に表示されます。仕入か期末在庫のどちらか一方のみが脱漏した場合に限り、当期純利益に影響が生じます。なおFOB shipping pointのため所有権は出荷時にLockhartへ移転しており、Year 1の資産・負債として計上すべきものです。

questions.subtopic: Inventory Errors - Combined Effect on Year 2 Income
questions.stem: Driscoll Co.'s Year 1 ending inventory was overstated by $30,000, and its Year 2 ending inventory was understated by $12,000. Neither error was discovered until Year 3. As a result of these errors, Driscoll's Year 2 net income was:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $42,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Understated by $42,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $18,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $18,000

questions.explanation: The Year 1 ending inventory overstatement carries into Year 2 as an overstated beginning inventory, which overstates Year 2 cost of goods sold and understates Year 2 income by $30,000. The Year 2 ending inventory understatement also overstates Year 2 cost of goods sold, understating income by another $12,000. The combined effect is income understated by $30,000 + $12,000 = $42,000. The $18,000 amounts result from incorrectly netting the two errors ($30,000 − $12,000) instead of recognizing that both push Year 2 income in the same direction.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1期末在庫の過大計上$30,000はYear 2の期首在庫の過大となり、Year 2の売上原価を過大・利益を$30,000過少にします。さらにYear 2期末在庫の過少計上$12,000も売上原価を過大・利益を$12,000過少にします。両者はYear 2の利益を同じ方向（過少）に動かすため、合計$30,000＋$12,000＝$42,000の過少表示です。$18,000は2つの誤りを相殺（$30,000−$12,000）した誤りで、両誤りがともに利益を押し下げる点を見落としています。

questions.subtopic: Purchase Commitment - Additional Loss in Year of Purchase
questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, Eastman Co. entered into a noncancelable commitment to purchase 50,000 units of raw material at $8.00 per unit during Year 2. At December 31, Year 1, the market price was $7.40 per unit, and Eastman properly accrued a loss. When Eastman purchased the units in Year 2, the market price had fallen to $7.10 per unit. What additional loss should Eastman recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: At December 31, Year 1, Eastman accrued a loss of ($8.00 − $7.40) × 50,000 = $30,000. When the goods are purchased in Year 2 at a market price of $7.10, the inventory is recorded at $7.10 per unit, and the additional decline of ($7.40 − $7.10) × 50,000 = $15,000 is recognized as a loss in Year 2. $45,000 is the total decline from the contract price, which double-counts the $30,000 already accrued in Year 1; $30,000 is the Year 1 loss, not the Year 2 loss; $0 would be correct only if the market price had not declined further after year-end.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 330-10-35-17により、解約不能な確定購入契約の市場価格下落損失は発生期に認識します。Year 1末に（$8.00−$7.40）×50,000＝$30,000の損失を計上済みです。Year 2の購入時、棚卸資産は市場価格$7.10で計上し、追加下落分（$7.40−$7.10）×50,000＝$15,000をYear 2の損失として認識します。$45,000は契約価格からの下落総額でありYear 1計上済みの$30,000を二重計上する誤り、$30,000はYear 1の損失額、$0は期末後に追加下落がなかった場合にのみ正しい値です。

questions.stem: On July 1, Quigley Co. signs a noncancelable contract to purchase 100,000 pounds of a commodity at a fixed price over the next 18 months. At the date the contract is signed, the contract price equals the market price, and the commitment is material. How should Quigley account for the commitment at inception?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record an asset and a liability equal to the total contract price

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record a liability for the total contract price with a corresponding deferred charge

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a loss for the full contract price as a contingency

choices.label: D
choices.text: Make no journal entry, but disclose the commitment in the notes to the financial statements

questions.explanation: A firm purchase commitment is an executory contract: neither party has performed at inception, so no asset or liability is recorded. Because the commitment is material, it must be disclosed in the notes. A loss is recognized only if, and when, the market price falls below the contract price on a noncancelable commitment. Recording an asset and liability, or a liability with a deferred charge, would improperly recognize an unexecuted exchange, and no loss exists at inception because contract and market prices are equal.
questions.explanationJa: 確定購入契約は未履行契約（executory contract）であり、契約締結時点では双方とも履行していないため資産・負債は計上しません。重要な契約であるため注記での開示が必要です（ASC 440-10-50）。損失は、解約不能な契約について市場価格が契約価格を下回った場合にのみ、その時点で認識します（ASC 330-10-35-17）。資産・負債の両建て計上や繰延費用を伴う負債計上は未履行の交換取引を不適切に認識するものであり、締結時は契約価格＝市場価格のため損失も存在しません。

questions.subtopic: Precious Metals - NRV Measurement Above Cost
questions.stem: Auric Mining Co. produces gold that has an immediate marketability at a quoted market price, units that are interchangeable, and insignificant costs of disposal. At year-end, Auric holds 1,000 ounces of gold with a production cost of $900 per ounce. The quoted market price is $1,300 per ounce, and estimated disposal costs are $20 per ounce. At what amount may Auric report this gold inventory?
questions.explanation: ASC 330 permits inventories of precious metals (and certain agricultural products) to be stated above cost at net realizable value when there is an immediate marketability at a quoted price, unit interchangeability, and an inability or impracticality of determining appropriate approximate costs — with the related income recognized at production. NRV is 1,000 × ($1,300 − $20) = $1,280,000. $900,000 is historical cost, which is not required for qualifying precious metals; $1,300,000 ignores the $20 per ounce disposal costs; $380,000 is the holding gain (NRV minus cost), not the carrying amount.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 330-10-35-15〜16では、即時に市場で換金可能な公表価格があり、単位が互換的で、処分費用が僅少な貴金属等の棚卸資産は、原価を超えるNRVで計上することが認められ、関連する利益は生産時点で認識されます。NRV＝1,000オンス×（$1,300−$20）＝$1,280,000です。$900,000は取得原価であり、要件を満たす貴金属には強制されません。$1,300,000は処分費用$20/オンスの控除漏れ、$380,000は保有利得（NRV−原価）であって帳簿価額ではありません。

questions.subtopic: FOB Destination - Title Transfer and Freight Classification
questions.stem: Maddox Co. sells goods to a customer with shipping terms of FOB destination and pays the freight charges. Which of the following statements is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Title passes to the customer when the goods are shipped, and the freight is a cost of inventory

choices.label: B
choices.text: Title passes to the customer upon delivery, and Maddox reports the freight as a delivery (selling) expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Title passes upon delivery, and the customer should capitalize the freight as part of its inventory cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Title passes when the goods are shipped, and the customer reimburses the freight as freight-in

questions.explanation: Under FOB destination terms, title and risk of loss remain with the seller until the goods reach the buyer, so the goods stay in Maddox's inventory while in transit. Freight paid by the seller on goods sold (freight-out) is a delivery or selling expense of the seller, not an inventoriable cost. The buyer does not capitalize freight it did not incur, and title does not pass at shipment under destination terms — that treatment applies to FOB shipping point.
questions.explanationJa: FOB destination（仕向地渡し）では、商品が買手に到達するまで所有権と滅失リスクは売手に残るため、輸送中の商品はMaddoxの棚卸資産です。売手が負担する販売商品の運賃（freight-out）は売手の配送費・販売費であり、棚卸資産原価には算入しません。買手は自ら負担していない運賃を資産化せず、出荷時点での所有権移転はFOB shipping pointの場合の取扱いです。

questions.subtopic: Net Method - Classification of Discounts Lost
questions.stem: Hobart Co. records purchases using the net method. During the year, Hobart failed to pay several invoices within the discount period. How should the purchase discounts lost be reported in Hobart's income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As an addition to the cost of inventory purchased

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a reduction of net sales

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a financing (other) expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an addition to cost of goods sold for the units sold

questions.explanation: Under the net method, purchases are recorded at the invoice price less the available cash discount, on the theory that the net price is the true cost of the goods. When a discount is missed, the extra amount paid is a cost of delaying payment — a financing cost — and is reported as purchase discounts lost, an other (financing) expense. It is not added to inventory or cost of goods sold, because the goods themselves are worth only the net price, and it has no relationship to sales revenue.
questions.explanationJa: ネット法では、現金割引控除後の正味価額が商品の真の原価であるという考え方に基づき、仕入を割引後価額で記録します。割引期間内に支払わなかった場合の追加支払額は支払を遅らせたことによる金融費用であり、「仕入割引逸失（purchase discounts lost）」としてその他（金融）費用に計上します。商品の価値は正味価額のままであるため棚卸資産や売上原価には加算せず、売上高とも無関係です。

questions.subtopic: LIFO Reserve - Converting COGS to FIFO Basis
questions.stem: Norcross Co. uses LIFO and reported cost of goods sold of $900,000 for the year. The LIFO reserve was $60,000 at the beginning of the year and $85,000 at the end of the year. What would Norcross's cost of goods sold have been under FIFO?
questions.explanation: FIFO cost of goods sold equals LIFO cost of goods sold minus the increase in the LIFO reserve during the year: $900,000 − ($85,000 − $60,000) = $900,000 − $25,000 = $875,000. An increasing reserve means LIFO charged more of the current (higher) costs to cost of goods sold than FIFO would have. $925,000 incorrectly adds the $25,000 increase; $840,000 incorrectly subtracts the entire beginning reserve; $815,000 incorrectly subtracts the entire ending reserve rather than only the change for the year.
questions.explanationJa: FIFOベースの売上原価＝LIFOベースの売上原価−当期のLIFOリザーブ増加額です。$900,000−（$85,000−$60,000）＝$900,000−$25,000＝$875,000となります。リザーブの増加は、LIFOがFIFOより多くの直近（高い）原価を売上原価に計上したことを意味します。$925,000は増加額$25,000を加算した誤り、$840,000は期首リザーブ全額を、$815,000は期末リザーブ全額を控除した誤りで、調整に用いるのは当期の増減額のみです。

questions.subtopic: Sales Cutoff Error - FOB Destination Shipment
questions.stem: On December 30, Year 1, Pemberton Co. shipped goods to a customer FOB destination and recorded the sale of $65,000 (cost $40,000) in Year 1. The goods were delivered to the customer on January 4, Year 2, and were excluded from Pemberton's December 31 physical count. By what amount is Pemberton's Year 1 net income misstated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $25,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $65,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Overstated by $40,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not misstated

questions.explanation: Under FOB destination terms, control does not transfer until delivery on January 4, Year 2, so the Year 1 sale should not have been recorded. Year 1 revenue is overstated by $65,000 and cost of goods sold is overstated by $40,000 (the goods should still be in inventory), so net income is overstated by $65,000 − $40,000 = $25,000. $65,000 considers only the revenue side; $40,000 considers only the cost side; the misstatement does not self-correct within Year 1, so income is not correctly stated.
questions.explanationJa: FOB destinationでは支配の移転は引渡日（Year 2の1月4日）であり、Year 1に売上を計上すべきではありません。Year 1の売上高は$65,000過大、売上原価は$40,000過大（商品はYear 1末時点でPembertonの棚卸資産に含めるべき）であり、純利益は$65,000−$40,000＝$25,000過大となります。$65,000は収益面のみ、$40,000は原価面のみを考慮した誤りです。この誤りはYear 1内では相殺されないため「影響なし」も誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Consignment - Consignor Profit on Consignment Sales
questions.stem: Stanfield Co. (consignor) ships 600 units costing $25 each to a consignee and pays $600 of freight to deliver the goods to the consignee. During the year, the consignee sells 400 units at $50 each, charges a commission of 10% of sales, and incurs $500 of reimbursable advertising costs on Stanfield's behalf. What profit should Stanfield recognize on the consignment sales for the year?
questions.explanation: Freight to the consignee is an inventoriable cost, so each unit costs $25 + ($600 ÷ 600) = $26. Sales are 400 × $50 = $20,000. Cost of goods sold is 400 × $26 = $10,400, the commission is $20,000 × 10% = $2,000, and the reimbursable advertising of $500 is an expense of the consignor. Profit = $20,000 − $10,400 − $2,000 − $500 = $7,100. $7,600 omits the advertising; $7,500 omits the freight component of cost of goods sold ($20,000 − $10,000 − $2,000 − $500); $9,100 omits the commission.
